items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: Bosz doet boekje open over Ajax-tijd: 'Van der Sar vond beker totaal onbelangrijk'
items.summary: Peter Bosz ziet de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker in Nederland steeds meer waarde krijgen. De 62-jarige PSV-trainer vertelde dat dit vroeger in zijn tijd bij Ajax wel even anders was.

items.title: VI Live: AZ neemt het in knock-outfase Youth League op tegen Dortmund
items.summary: Dinsdagavond staat de eerste wedstrijd in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. Om 20.00 uur staan AZ en FC Twente tegenover elkaar. Welke ploeg zich als eerst halvefinalist mag noemen, volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: In deze landen is de transfermarkt langer open
items.summary: Terwijl in de topcompetities de winterse transfermarkt al gesloten is, zijn er diverse landen waarin clubs nog spelers kunnen halen. Onder meer Nederland gaat een dag langer door. Een overzicht van de latere transferdeadlines.

items.title: 'I've missed the buzz of competing' - Draper set for Davis Cup return
items.summary: The latest news around British men's tennis No 1 Jack Draper as he prepares to return to action in February after an arm injury.

items.title: Deadline Day: Letschert van China naar AFC, weinig vuurwerk in Eindhoven
items.summary: Het is Deadline Day in Nederland, voor de VriendenLoterij Eredivisie en Keuken Kampioen Divisie. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers op de laatste transferdag van Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland , waar het gisteren Deadline Day was.

items.title: Ukraine chief blasts Infantino for Russia remark
items.summary: Ukraine's sports minister has said FIFA president Gianni Infantino's bid to lift a ban on Russia competing at youth level are "irresponsible" and "infantile."

items.title: Liverpool seal $74M deal for Jacquet from Rennes
items.summary: Liverpool have confirmed the signing of defender Jérémy Jacquet from Rennes who is set to join the club in the summer.

items.title: Benzema joins Al Hilal as Kante heads to Fener
items.summary: Karim Benzema has left Al Ittihad and signed a contract with Saudi Pro League rivals Al Hilal, the club announced Monday.

items.title: Crystal Palace accused of messing with Dwight McNeil’s mental health after transfer U-turn
items.summary: Palace did not sign Everton winger after he had medical McNeil’s partner criticises ‘cruel’ football world Dwight McNeil’s partner has claimed Crystal Palace provided no explanation for pulling out of a move to sign him from Everton on transfer deadline day and accused them of toying with the winger’s mental health. McNeil stayed at Everton after Palace decided not to proceed with a £20m deal at the last minute despite the 26-year-old having completed his medical and agreed terms on a four-and-a-half-year contract. It is understood the collapse of Jean-Philippe Mateta’s £30m move to Milan, after the France striker failed a medical, prompted Palace to change their offer to an initial loan with an obligation to buy at the end of the season. Continue reading...

Howe: Tonali committed to Newcastle amid reported Arsenal interest
items.summary: Newcastle head coach Eddie Howe says he does not see Sandro Tonali becoming "unsettled" amid renewed transfer interest.

items.title: Palace criticised for 'messing' with McNeil mental health

items.title: Tonali 'very happy' at Newcastle amid Arsenal links
items.summary: Manager Eddie Howe says Sandro Tonali is "very happy" at Newcastle United after the midfielder was linked with a move to Arsenal.

items.title: Transfer window verdict: how every Premier League club fared
items.summary: Will Arsenal regret Nwaneri move? Have Sunderland traded brilliantly again? We run the rule over every team’s business The foot injury sustained by Mikel Merino made the last few days of the window a bit more interesting for Arsenal supporters, although in the end there was no big signing. Deadline-day links to Sandro Tonali of Newcastle and Leon Goretzka came to nothing, and Arsenal missed out to their north London rivals Tottenham on the 18-year-old Scotland striker James Wilson. They did sign the England Under-19 defender Jaden Dixon from Stoke but will Mikel Arteta regret allowing Ethan Nwaneri to join Marseille on loan with Merino poised to be out for at least two months? Ed Aarons Continue reading...

items.title: Doohan joins Haas as reserve driver
items.summary: Haas have signed Jack Doohan as a reserve driver for the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: Infantino 'infantile' for wanting to lift Russia ban
items.summary: Ukraine's sports minister calls Fifa president Gianni Infantino "irresponsible" and "infantile" for saying it will look at lifting a ban on Russia.
